title: Today's AI Brief: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
summary: Microsoft and OpenAI part ways, Trump admin struggles with AI regulation, and AI agent development heats up.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
imageCaption: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight (theverge.com)

body.text: I've been keeping an eye on the AI space, and this week, some interesting developments caught my attention. It seems like Microsoft and OpenAI have gone their separate ways, and now they're gearing up to compete with each other.

According to a report from The Verge, Microsoft and OpenAI have parted ways, and Microsoft is now building its own AI agents from the ground up. This move signals a significant shift in the AI landscape, as Microsoft's AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman, emphasized the need to prove that they can do everything they need to without relying on OpenAI. This development raises questions about the economics of AI models and how companies are approaching data boundaries and safety.

Next, I came across an article from Wired about the Trump administration's internal struggle over AI regulation. It appears that there's a power struggle within the administration about whether to revive an executive order on AI regulation that President Trump abruptly canceled last month. This internal conflict highlights the challenges of regulating AI and the difficulties in finding a balance between innovation and oversight.

Also on the board, The Verge reported that Microsoft is investing heavily in building its own AI agents, which could potentially compete with OpenAI's offerings. This move could lead to a more diverse and competitive AI landscape, which could benefit users and drive adoption.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
